_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PANEL
name: Ledgerly Bookkeeping
author: dave.bird@outlook.com
description: Bookkeeping business website CMS. Edit content online at /admin/, generate static HTML for deployment. Flask + SQLite + Frozen-Flask.

startup_commands.default: export PATH=/home/container/.local/bin:$PATH; git config --global --add safe.directory /home/container 2>/dev/null; if [[ -d .git ]] && [[ "{{AUTO_UPDATE}}" == "1" ]]; then echo '--- Auto-update: pulling latest ---'; export GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0; _ADDR="{{GIT_ADDRESS}}"; if [[ -n "{{ACCESS_TOKEN}}" ]] && [[ -n "{{USERNAME}}" ]]; then _ADDR="https://{{USERNAME}}:{{ACCESS_TOKEN}}@$(echo "{{GIT_ADDRESS}}" | cut -d/ -f3-)"; fi; if ! git remote get-url origin >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'No remote found, adding origin...'; git remote add origin "${_ADDR}"; else git remote set-url origin "${_ADDR}"; fi; _BRANCH="{{BRANCH}}"; _BRANCH="${_BRANCH:-main}"; git fetch --all && git reset --hard "origin/${_BRANCH}" && echo '--- Update complete ---' || echo '--- Update FAILED ---'; fi; if [[ -f /home/container/{{REQUIREMENTS_FILE}} ]]; then pip install -U --prefix .local -r {{REQUIREMENTS_FILE}}; fi; find . -type d -name __pycache__ -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true; bash startup.sh

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# Ledgerly Bookkeeping Installation Script
#
# Server Files: /mnt/server
apt update
apt install -y git curl jq file unzip make gcc g++ libtool

mkdir -p /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server

## Fix git ownership check
git config --global --add safe.directory /mnt/server

echo "============================================"
echo "Installing Ledgerly Bookkeeping"
echo "============================================"

if [ "${USER_UPLOAD}" == "true" ] || [ "${USER_UPLOAD}" == "1" ]; then
    echo -e "User upload mode - skipping git clone"
    exit 0
fi

## add git ending if it's not on the address
if [[ ${GIT_ADDRESS} != *.git ]]; then
    GIT_ADDRESS=${GIT_ADDRESS}.git
fi

if [ -z "${USERNAME}" ] && [ -z "${ACCESS_TOKEN}" ]; then
    echo -e "Using anonymous git clone"
else
    GIT_ADDRESS="https://${USERNAME}:${ACCESS_TOKEN}@$(echo -e ${GIT_ADDRESS} | cut -d/ -f3-)"
fi

## pull git python repo
if [ "$(ls -A /mnt/server)" ]; then
    echo -e "/mnt/server directory is not empty."
    if [ -d .git ]; then
        echo -e ".git directory exists"
        if [ -f .git/config ]; then
            echo -e "Loading info from git config"
            ORIGIN=$(git config --get remote.origin.url)
        else
            echo -e "Files found with no git config"
            echo -e "Closing out without touching things to not break anything"
            exit 10
        fi
    else
        echo -e ".git directory missing - initializing git repo"
        git init -b main
        git remote add origin ${GIT_ADDRESS}
        ORIGIN=${GIT_ADDRESS}
    fi

    if [ "${ORIGIN}" == "${GIT_ADDRESS}" ]; then
        echo "Pulling latest from git repo"
        git fetch --all
        _BRANCH="${BRANCH:-main}"
        git reset --hard "origin/${_BRANCH}"
    fi
else
    echo -e "/mnt/server is empty.\nCloning files from repo"
    if [ -z ${BRANCH} ]; then
        echo -e "Cloning default branch"
        git clone ${GIT_ADDRESS} .
    else
        echo -e "Cloning ${BRANCH}"
        git clone --single-branch --branch ${BRANCH} ${GIT_ADDRESS} .
    fi
fi

export HOME=/mnt/server

echo ""
echo "[1/1] Pip dependencies will be installed on first startup."

echo ""
echo "============================================"
echo "Installation complete!"
echo "============================================"
echo ""
echo "IMPORTANT: Set these environment variables:"
echo "  - ADMIN_PASSWORD (default: changeme - change immediately!)"
echo "  - SECRET_KEY     (recommended - Flask session secret)"
echo ""
echo "Generate SECRET_KEY with:"
echo "  python -c 'import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))'"
echo "============================================"
exit 0

variables.name: Admin Password
variables.description: Password to access the /admin/ panel. Change from the default immediately after first login.

variables.name: Secret Key
variables.description: Flask session secret key. Generate with: python -c 'import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))'

variables.name: Database Path
variables.description: Path to the SQLite database file.

variables.name: Server Port
variables.description: Port the application listens on. Pelican sets this automatically.

variables.name: Git Repo Address
variables.description: The URL of your git repository (e.g. https://github.com/youruser/ledgerlyfrome)

variables.name: Git Branch
variables.description: Branch to deploy. Leave blank to use the default branch (main).

variables.name: Git Username
variables.description: Git username for private repositories. Leave blank for public repos.

variables.name: Git Access Token
variables.description: Personal access token for private repositories. Leave blank for public repos.

variables.name: Auto Update
variables.description: Pull latest code from git on each startup. Set to 1 to enable, 0 to disable.

variables.name: Requirements File
variables.description: Path to the pip requirements file.

variables.name: User Uploaded Files
variables.description: Set to 1 if you have manually uploaded files and want to skip the git clone during install.

variables.name: Mail Server
variables.description: SMTP server hostname for outgoing email.

variables.name: Mail Username
variables.description: Microsoft 365 mailbox username (usually full email address).

variables.name: Mail Password
variables.description: Microsoft 365 mailbox password or app password for SMTP AUTH.
